_description: Expected orchestrator behavior for each of the 3 required chaos scenarios.

scenario_1.name: Action success — latency inject on payment-svc
expected_flow: DETECT: alert HighLatency fires on payment-svc within 60s of inject
DECIDE: map HighLatency → restart_service.sh; blast-radius check passes
DRY_RUN: restart_service.sh --dry-run --service payment-svc → exit 0
ACT: restart_service.sh --service payment-svc → container restarts
VERIFY: poll Prometheus for 60s; p99 latency drops below 500ms threshold
SUCCESS: log event_type=ACTION_SUCCESS

scenario_2.name: Action fail → rollback — checkout-svc killed
scenario_2.note: To force verify FAIL for testing rollback logic: temporarily set verify_thresholds.latency_p99_max_ms=1 in config so verify always fails even after successful restart.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert InstanceDown fires on checkout-svc within 60s of kill
DECIDE: map InstanceDown → restart_service.sh; blast-radius check passes
DRY_RUN: restart_service.sh --dry-run --service checkout-svc → exit 0
ACT: restart_service.sh --service checkout-svc → container starts
VERIFY: poll Prometheus 60s; verify FAILS (service still unhealthy or threshold forced low)
ROLLBACK: auto-trigger rollback action
LOG: event_type=ROLLBACK_TRIGGERED; failure_count incremented to 1

scenario_3.name: Circuit breaker — 3 consecutive failures
scenario_3.setup: Run scenario_2 style inject 3 times in a row with verify always failing (keep low threshold). Each run increments failure_count.
expected_flow: Failure 1: action executed, verify fail, rollback triggered — failure_count=1
Failure 2: action executed, verify fail, rollback triggered — failure_count=2
Failure 3: action executed, verify fail, rollback triggered — failure_count=3
CIRCUIT_BREAKER: failure_count >= 3 → orchestrator logs CIRCUIT_BREAKER_HALT
HALT: no further actions executed until circuit is manually reset

scenario_4_blast_radius.name: Blast-radius limit exceeded — rate limiting enforcement
scenario_4_blast_radius.description: Trigger 3 alerts within 60 seconds when max_actions_per_minute is set to 2. The 3rd alert should be detected but rejected due to blast-radius limit.
scenario_4_blast_radius.setup: Set blast_radius.max_actions_per_minute: 2 in config.yaml. Inject faults on 3 different services rapidly.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert HighLatency on payment-svc → blast-radius check passes (1/2)
ACTION: restart payment-svc → ACTION_SUCCESS
DETECT: alert HighLatency on inventory-svc → blast-radius check passes (2/2)
ACTION: restart inventory-svc → ACTION_SUCCESS
DETECT: alert HighLatency on checkout-svc → blast-radius EXCEEDED (3/2)
ESCALATE: log BLAST_RADIUS_EXCEEDED, action=escalate, do NOT execute runbook
GAUGE: blast_radius_remaining metric decrements to 0
scenario_4_blast_radius.observable_outcome: Log shows first 2 actions complete successfully. Third alert logs BLAST_RADIUS_EXCEEDED with action=escalate. No runbook execution for 3rd alert. Prometheus gauge closed_loop_blast_radius_remaining goes from 2 → 1 → 0.

scenario_5_cascading_failure.name: Cascading failure recovery — upstream service down triggers downstream errors
scenario_5_cascading_failure.description: api-gateway goes down, causing HighErrorRate on downstream services (payment-svc, inventory-svc). Orchestrator restarts api-gateway first, downstream services auto-recover.
scenario_5_cascading_failure.setup: Kill api-gateway. Wait for downstream error alerts. Verify orchestrator prioritizes root cause.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert InstanceDown on api-gateway fires first (within 30s)
DECIDE: map InstanceDown → restart_service.sh for api-gateway
ACTION: restart api-gateway → container starts
VERIFY: api-gateway up metric returns to 1 → VERIFY_PASS
DETECT: HighErrorRate alerts may fire on payment-svc / inventory-svc (30-60s after kill)
ACTION: if alerts still active, execute runbooks on downstream services
VERIFY: after api-gateway recovery, downstream error rates drop → VERIFY_PASS or alerts auto-resolve
LOG: demonstrates handling of cascade: root cause fixed first, downstream follows
scenario_5_cascading_failure.observable_outcome: api-gateway restart completes first with ACTION_SUCCESS. Downstream services either auto-recover (no action needed) or get ACTION_SUCCESS after runbook execution. No circular restart loops. Per-service mutex prevents redundant actions on same service.

scenario_6_verify_timeout.name: Verify polling resilience — service recovers mid-verify window
scenario_6_verify_timeout.description: Inject latency, execute action, then manually recover service during verify polling. Verify step must handle partial samples and require min_samples consecutive passes.
scenario_6_verify_timeout.setup: Set verify_timeout_seconds: 30 and verify_min_samples: 3 in config.yaml. Inject fault, then recover manually after 10 seconds.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert HighLatency on payment-svc
ACTION: restart payment-svc → runbook executes
VERIFY: start polling Prometheus every 10s for 30s total
VERIFY poll 1: may still show high latency (service not yet recovered) → FAIL sample
VERIFY poll 2: service recovered manually → latency < threshold → PASS sample 1/3
VERIFY poll 3: latency < threshold → PASS sample 2/3
VERIFY poll 4: latency < threshold → PASS sample 3/3 → VERIFY_PASS
LOG: VERIFY_PASS with sample_count=3, demonstrates verify handles recovery timing
scenario_6_verify_timeout.observable_outcome: Log shows multiple VERIFY_POLLING events with metric values. Early polls may fail, later polls pass. VERIFY_PASS appears only after verify_min_samples consecutive passes. Demonstrates resilience to timing edge cases during service recovery.

scenario_7_multi_step_rollback.name: Transactional rollback chain — multi_step_deploy step-C fails
scenario_7_multi_step_rollback.description: A 3-step deploy (A→B→C) is triggered. Step C fails. Orchestrator must rollback completed steps in reverse order: rollback-B then rollback-A.
scenario_7_multi_step_rollback.setup: Add MultiStepDeploy to runbook_map and multi_step_map in config.yaml pointing to multi_step_deploy.sh steps. Force step-C to fail by passing an invalid service name or removing the container before step-C.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert MultiStepDeploy fires
DECIDE: map MultiStepDeploy → multi_step_deploy.sh; validation passes
DRY_RUN: dry-run passes for first step
ACT step-A: multi_step_deploy.sh --step-a → exit 0; logged TRANSACTIONAL_STEP complete
ACT step-B: multi_step_deploy.sh --step-b → exit 0; logged TRANSACTIONAL_STEP complete
ACT step-C: multi_step_deploy.sh --step-c → exit 1; logged TRANSACTIONAL_STEP_FAIL
ROLLBACK: rollback-B executed (reverse of step-B); logged TRANSACTIONAL_ROLLBACK_STEP
ROLLBACK: rollback-A executed (reverse of step-A); logged TRANSACTIONAL_ROLLBACK_STEP
LOG: TRANSACTIONAL_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE with rolled_back=[rollback-B, rollback-A]
scenario_7_multi_step_rollback.observable_outcome: Log shows exactly 2 rollback steps in reverse order (rollback-B before rollback-A). No partial state left on the service.

scenario_8_concurrent_alert.name: Concurrent alert race — 2 alerts fire on different services simultaneously
scenario_8_concurrent_alert.description: payment-svc and inventory-svc both fire HighLatency at the same time. Orchestrator serializes per service (mutex keyed by service name) but allows both services to proceed in parallel.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert HighLatency fires on payment-svc AND inventory-svc (same poll cycle)
LOCK: SERVICE_LOCK acquired for payment-svc; SERVICE_LOCK acquired for inventory-svc (different locks → both proceed)
ACT: runbook executes on payment-svc concurrently with runbook on inventory-svc
If a second alert for payment-svc arrives while first is still executing: log SERVICE_LOCK_BUSY and skip
VERIFY + SUCCESS on both services independently
scenario_8_concurrent_alert.observable_outcome: Timestamps in log show both services' DRY_RUN_PASS events within the same second. No SERVICE_LOCK_BUSY between different services. If same service fires twice, second alert logs SERVICE_LOCK_BUSY.

scenario_9_decision_validation.name: LLM hallucination defense — decide returns non-existent runbook
scenario_9_decision_validation.description: The runbook_map (or LLM decide path) returns a runbook name that is not present in runbook_registry. Orchestrator must refuse execution, log audit event, and escalate.
scenario_9_decision_validation.setup: Temporarily add a mapping in runbook_map pointing alertname 'TestHallucination' to 'runbooks/nonexistent_runbook.sh'. Ensure runbook_registry does NOT include that path. Inject a synthetic alert with alertname=TestHallucination.
expected_flow: DETECT: alert TestHallucination detected
DECIDE: runbook_map returns 'runbooks/nonexistent_runbook.sh'
VALIDATION: validate_runbook checks registry — name absent
LOG: DECISION_VALIDATION_FAILED with bad_runbook + alertname + raw_decision + action=escalate_no_auto_action
HALT: process_alert returns immediately; no DRY_RUN, no ACT, no runbook executed
scenario_9_decision_validation.observable_outcome: Log contains DECISION_VALIDATION_FAILED with field bad_runbook='runbooks/nonexistent_runbook.sh'. No runbook subprocess is spawned. No RUNBOOK_EXEC event appears.
